evidence_excerpt: ESES resolved with treatment (control within months to ~1 year), EEG normalized by age 16 with no seizure recurrence, but Kanji dysgraphia only ameliorated gradually and both patients "remained weak in [the] ability to write Kanji at high school."

quality_of_life.evidence_excerpt: Ongoing but improved cognitive symptom: Kanji writing remained weak at high school despite control of ESES and normalized EEG.

timeline_events.evidence_excerpt: Patient 1: "Four months were required to control ESES, although spikes remained in the left occipital region."
Patient 2: "Approximately one year was required to control ESES."
Patient 2: After multiple ineffective agents, "Adrenocorticotropic hormone treatment markedly decreased generalized CSW, and control of ESES was maintained by sodium valproate and clorazepate."
Patient 1: "At 16 years of age, EEG was normalized, and clinical seizure and epileptic discharge did not recur even without antiepileptic drugs."
Patient 2: "At 16 years of age, EEG was normalized, and clinical seizure and epileptic discharge did not recur with sodium valproate alone."
Both patients: "Kanji dysgraphia ameliorated gradually several years after CSW was controlled, but he remained weak in his ability to write Kanji at high school."

notes_on_uncertainty: Report describes two patients. Outcomes are summarized across both; precise dates are not provided. Index time is approximated as the time ESES was diagnosed by overnight EEG and treatment initiated. Long-term refers to follow-up into high school age (~years after diagnosis). No deaths or major complications were reported.
